100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 15, 1926, Britain faced rising tensions as the General Strike continued to strain relations between workers and the government, with calls for negotiations intensifying. Meanwhile, in the United States, a significant heatwave gripped the Midwest, leading to health concerns and increased demand for relief efforts. Internationally, the League of Nations convened discussions on disarmament, reflecting ongoing efforts to maintain peace in a post-war world.
